# Design Tokens

**Design tokens** represent the core visual elements (such as colors, typography, and spacing) of a design system. They ensure consistency across platforms, devices, and tech stacks by storing these elements in a platform-agnostic format.

To learn more, review the [MADE Surface best practices and guidelines](https://made.mastercard.com/foundations/design-tokens/).

## Design Token Lists for Mastercard and Unify Themes

Explore the complete set of design tokens for Mastercard and Unify themes, available in both light and dark modes:

- **Mastercard Theme**: [View Mastercard Design Tokens](docs/mastercard-design-tokens.html)
- **Unify Theme**: [View Unify Design Tokens](docs/unify-design-tokens.html)


## Supported Formats

MADE design tokens are available in a platform-agnostic JSON format and can be transformed into various other formats using [Style Dictionary](https://amzn.github.io/style-dictionary/#/). This enables you to scale design tokens across different platforms and languages. Available formats include:

| Format | Description             |
| ------ | ----------------------- |
| JSON   | Platform-agnostic base  |
| TS   | For TypeScript-based base  |
| CSS    | For CSS-based projects  |
| SCSS   | For Sass-based projects |
| LESS   | For Less-based projects |
| Swift  | For iOS development     |
| XML    | For Android development |

---

## Getting Started with MADE Design Tokens

Follow the steps below to get started with MADE Design Tokens in your project within the Mastercard network.

### Prerequisites

Ensure **Node.js** is installed. If not, download the latest version via Self Service. Visit the [Node.js website](https://nodejs.org/) for guidance.

Verify Node.js installation with:

```sh
node -v
npm -v
```

---

## Installation

MADE Design Tokens can be installed via the **Mastercard Artifactory** or **Forge Artifactory**. To install, configure your registry to point to the `npm-all` repository in Artifactory.

### Option 1: Configure globally (applies to all projects)

Set the registry for all npm operations:

#### Mastercard
```sh
npm config set registry https://artifacts.mastercard.int/artifactory/api/npm/npm-all
```

#### Forge
```sh
npm config set registry https://artifacts.forge.mastercard.com/artifactory/api/npm/npm-all
```

### Option 2: Configure temporarily (specific to the current command)

Use the `--registry` flag for a single installation:

#### Mastercard
```sh
npm install --registry https://artifacts.mastercard.int/artifactory/api/npm/npm-all
```

#### Forge
```sh
npm install --registry https://artifacts.forge.mastercard.com/artifactory/api/npm/npm-all
```

To install the MADE package, run the following in your project directory (where `package.json` is located), ensuring you're logged into the Mastercard firewall:

```sh
npm install @mc-made/design-tokens@3.0.0-beta
```

---

## Link to Assets

The following packages are available for usage:

```sh
https://made.mastercard.com/design-tokens/3.0.0-beta/themes/mastercard/light.[css|json|less|scss|ts|d.ts]
https://made.mastercard.com/design-tokens/3.0.0-beta/themes/mastercard/dark.[css|json|less|scss|ts|d.ts]
https://made.mastercard.com/design-tokens/3.0.0-beta/themes/unify/light.[css|json|less|scss|ts|d.ts]
https://made.mastercard.com/design-tokens/3.0.0-beta/themes/unify/dark.[css|json|less|scss|ts|d.ts]
```

---

## Integrating Design Tokens into Your Codebase

### Web 

#### CSS

To use design tokens in CSS, include the stylesheet `<link>` in your `<head>` before other styles:

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="[path-to-directory]/[theme]/[color-mode].css" />
```

Simply change theme file name to change to a new theme.

Use design tokens in your CSS:

```css
background-color: var(--made-color-brand-primary);
```

Simply change theme file name to change to a new theme.


```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="[path-to-directory]/mastercard/light.css" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="[path-to-directory]/mastercard/dark.css" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="[path-to-directory]/unify/light.css" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="[path-to-directory]/unify/dark.css" />
```

---

#### SCSS

To use design tokens in SCSS, add the following to the start of your main SCSS file:

```scss
@import "[path-to-directory]/[theme]/[color-mode].scss";
```

Use design tokens in your SCSS:

```scss
background-color: $made-color-brand-primary;
```

---

#### LESS

For LESS, add the following to your main LESS file:

```less
@import "[path-to-directory]/[theme]/[color-mode].less";
```

Use tokens in LESS:

```less
background-color: @made-color-brand-primary;
```

---

#### JavaScript (JSON)

Import design tokens into JavaScript as follows:

```js
import { DesignTokens } from "[path-to-directory]/[theme]/[color-mode].json";
```

In JSON, token names are PascalCase:

```js
backgroundColor: MadeColorBrandPrimary;
```

---

For iOS, please refer to the [README-IOS](README-IOS.md) file

For Android, please refer to the [README-ANDROID](android/README.md) file

### Extending with Design Tokens

Extend MADE components using design tokens. For example, modify a primary button’s appearance:

```css
.made-c-button--primary {
  background-color: var(--made-color-action-background-primary-default);
  border-color: var(--made-color-action-border-primary-default);
  border-radius: var(--made-button-border-radius);
  color: var(--made-color-action-text-on-primary);
  padding: 0 var(--made-space-8-x);
}
```

---

## Support

**Need a different format?**

If the format you need isn’t listed, please contact us via our [MS Teams support channel](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/team/19%3AtsxjLiEZXZYXKmSmj2KVuse6ORp7lEqRuAia5oRSHT01%40thread.tacv2/conversations?groupId=0e9079ff-99a3-407d-ad89-264c24587932&amp;tenantId=f06fa858-824b-4a85-aacb-f372cfdc282e) for assistance. You can also check the [Style Dictionary documentation](https://amzn.github.io/style-dictionary/#/formats?id=pre-defined-formats) for additional formats, naming conventions, and transformations.
